home.heroDesc: {brand} extracts match evidence from teams, players, injuries, tactics and head-to-head context, then generates a {agentScale} scenario matrix, scoreline probabilities, key event chain and replayable prediction report for {optimalSolution}.
home.slogan: Split the match into evidence, scenarios, events and risks, then make the prediction replayable
home.systemReadyDesc: Prediction engine on standby. Upload unstructured match data to initialize the football prediction flow.
home.step01Desc: Extract teams, players, tactics, injuries and match context into a football knowledge graph
home.step02Desc: Identify home/away teams, evidence scope, strength parameters and scenario spaces
home.step03Desc: Generate the 3x3 state matrix, six scenario spaces, score probabilities and match event chain
home.step04Desc: Create a replayable match prediction report from discussion evidence and match-event hypotheses
home.step05Desc: Ask follow-up questions about the report, score probabilities, scenario matrix and key risks
home.domainPublicOpinionDesc: Single-match W/D/L, scoreline and key-event projection
home.domainFootballMatchDesc: Men's single-match score and key events

step1.ontologyDesc: LLM analyzes match files and prediction requirements, extracting teams, players, matches, tactics and injury ontology structures
step1.graphRagDesc: Based on the generated ontology, documents are auto-chunked and sent to Zep to build a knowledge graph, extracting entities and relations, forming temporal memory and community summaries
step1.buildCompleteDesc: Match graph build is complete. Proceed to prediction setup.

step2.simInstanceDesc: Create a new simulation instance and pull world parameter templates
step2.generateAgentPersonaDesc: Combine context to auto-extract entities and relations from the knowledge graph, initialize simulated individuals, and assign unique behaviors and memories based on reality seeds
step2.dualPlatformConfigDesc: LLM sets match tempo, team states, scenario spaces, key-event triggers and risk perturbations from the prediction request and match files
step2.initialActivationDesc: Auto-generate initial activation events and hot topics based on narrative direction to guide the simulation world's initial state
step2.setupCompleteDesc: Simulation environment is ready. You can now start the simulation.
step2.roundsConfigDesc: GoalFish auto-plans to simulate {hours} real-world hours, each round representing {minutesPerRound} minutes of elapsed time
step2.customTip: For first-time runs, we strongly recommend switching to 'Custom Mode' to reduce rounds for a quick preview and lower error risk
step2.personaDimExperienceDesc: Complete behavioral trajectory in this event
step2.personaDimSocialDesc: Team, player, tactical and event relationship graph
step2.metricConfigText: Prediction config ID generated by Step 2. Step 3 uses it to read team strengths, scenario matrix, review records, and recovery policy.
step2.metricStatusText: pending means not complete, preparing means generating, ready means Step 3 can start, and failed/error means regeneration is needed.
step2.metricProgressText: Backend milestone progress covering roster matching, data-source reads, team-strength estimation, scenario design, and recovery nodes.
step2.metricModelText: Underlying football goal model for this config. Uses a fitted model when data is sufficient and falls back to priors or rules when data is limited.
step2.metricFitText: Shows whether model parameters were fitted from structured results. fitted is stronger; fallback_prior or insufficient means more reliance on roster, ranking, and graph evidence.
step2.metricRosterText: Total players available for modeling both teams. Roster data affects ratings, positional depth, injury risk, goalkeeper strength, and event participants.
step2.metricDataSourcesText: Synced sources / total sources. Includes international results, Elo, FIFA ranking, and optional xG data; failures use cache or fallback features.
step2.metricReviewText: Actual LLM calls / planned calls, with cap as the hard limit. fresh/cached are ledger values after generation; actual calls may be 0 while preparing or when LLM is disabled.
step2.sectionDataRosterText: Objective inputs used by the model: external results/ranking/xG snapshots for team strength, and player rosters for depth, position ability, injury status, and goalkeeper information.
step2.budgetLocked: This config is being generated, so the budget is locked. Wait for this run to finish before applying changes.
step2.coachJuryText: The system creates 100 coach agents, but only budget-enabled roles join key discussions. Roles review the same match from attack, defense, transition, set pieces, goalkeeper, fitness, and risk angles.
step2.scenarioDesignText: Splits the match into possible worlds such as baseline, home upside, away error, or volatility. Each scenario has initial and reviewed weights; Step 3 samples match flow by these weights.
step2.resumePolicyText: Defines which workflow nodes must persist and where interrupted or regenerated runs resume, so Step 3, report generation, and replay reuse one config without repeating costly work.
step2.resumeSequenceText: Execution order of prediction workflow nodes. Lower numbers are generated earlier and downstream nodes depend on them.
step2.resumeNodeText: Recoverable workflow step, such as team-strength generation, scenario matrix, match events, or report summary.
step2.resumePersistText: Yes means the node output is written to the database and can be reused after refresh, backend restart, or rerun.
step2.resumeStrategyText: How to handle the node after interruption: reuse existing output, recompute, or continue from the nearest stable node.
step2.teamStrengthText: Converts graph, roster, ranking, and results into 0-100 team dimension scores. Attack covers chance creation and finishing; defense covers chance prevention; possession covers buildup and tempo; transition covers counter-pressing and counterattack; set pieces cover aerial and second-ball strength; goalkeeper covers shot-stopping, distribution, and crosses.
step2.modelInputsText: Shows the key inputs and fallback reasons the model actually used, indicating whether results are strongly supported by structured data or rely mainly on report text, rosters, and priors.
step2.inputMatchText: Home/away teams and match name recognized from requirement text, graph entities, or explicit inputs. Wrong recognition affects roster matching and strength estimation.
step2.inputGraphText: Knowledge graph ID generated by Step 1. Step 2 reads teams, players, tactics, weather, injuries, and competition context from graph nodes.
step2.inputDataSufficiencyText: Overall input-quality judgment. sufficient means structured samples are fairly complete; partial means usable with gaps; insufficient means fallback logic matters.
step2.inputStructuredResultsText: Matched historical match records. More records make team strength and recent-form estimates more stable; fewer records fall back to rankings, rosters, and text evidence.
step2.inputXgSamplesText: Available expected-goals samples. xG adds chance-quality evidence beyond scorelines; the model still runs without it but attack/defense estimates are rougher.
step2.inputFallbackText: Alternative path used when structured data is sparse, model fitting fails, or external sources fail. Details show reasons and diagnostics.
step2.logStep2Regenerated: Step 2 reconfigured; old Step 3/Step 4/Step 5 artifacts are invalidated
step2.roleDesc_head_coach: Synthesizes specialist views into overall tactical judgment, baseline score tendency, and key match levers.
step2.roleDesc_attack: Focuses on chance creation, forward efficiency, wing progression, box touches, and key finishers.
step2.roleDesc_defense: Focuses on defensive structure, box protection, center-back duels, fullback space, and error risk.
step2.roleDesc_transition: Focuses on high press, counter-pressing, transition speed, second balls, and exposed spaces.
step2.roleDesc_set_piece: Focuses on corners, free kicks, far-post protection, aerial duels, and set-piece goal risk.
step2.roleDesc_goalkeeper: Focuses on shot-stopping, distribution, crosses, sweeping, and high-line protection.
step2.roleDesc_fitness: Focuses on fatigue, substitution windows, squad depth, travel/rest, and late-game output.
step2.roleDesc_risk: Focuses on referee style, card pressure, VAR/penalty risk, weather, and extreme events.
step2.configIdentityMismatch: Prediction config does not match the uploaded match.
step2.logConfigIdentityMismatch: Ignored stale prediction config with mismatched teams: {id}
step2.logLoadExistingConfigFailed: Failed to read existing prediction config: {error}

step3.oldSimCleared: Old simulation logs cleared, restarting simulation
step3.preparingGoBack: Preparing to return to Step 2, closing simulation...
step3.closeFailed: Failed to close simulation environment, attempting force stop...
step3.title: Match Scenario Simulation
step3.matrixEmpty: Start from the Step 2 config to show the nine-scenario matrix.
step3.emptyEventChain: No event chain for this scenario.
step3.reviewFootnote: Confidence adjustment {delta}; Step 3 review checks only the event chain and modal trajectory, and does not override scoreline probabilities.
step3.emptyFallbacks: No narrative fallback events for this scenario.
step3.consensusExplain: Consensus score weights role review verdicts: support=1, watch/adjust=0.5, reject=0, then divides by participating review roles.
step3.budgetExplain: Budget numbers come from the current prediction run LLM ledger. total_calls are completed recorded calls, hard_cap is the Step 3 hard limit; over-limit steps fall back.
step3.phaseQueued: Match simulation is queued in Celery
step3.phaseRunning: Generating score probabilities, event chain, and review results
step3.emptyCoachReviewTooltip: No Step 3 coach review for the current scenario
step3.coachReviewTooltip: Source: {formula} = {label}; click to view role verdicts
step3.budgetTooltip: LLM calls {used}; remaining {remaining}; click for role and fallback details
step3.logNoActiveRun: No active Step 3 run for this project; cleared old prediction_run_id from route
step3.missingStep2Config: Missing active Step 2 config. Complete Step 2 first.
step3.logOldRunSwitched: Old Step 3 run from route is invalid; switched to current active run
step3.logActiveWorkflowFailed: Failed to read project active workflow: {error}
step3.logRestoreConfigFailed: Failed to restore config from Step 3 run status: {error}
step3.logStep3Regenerated: Step 3 regenerated; old Step 4 report and Step 5 Q&A are invalidated
step3.queueEntering: Match simulation is entering Celery queue
step3.logComplete: Nine-scenario match simulation complete; all artifacts persisted
step3.logStartReport: Starting match prediction report from nine-scenario artifacts
step3.logReportFailed: Failed to generate match prediction report: {error}
step3.missingConfigError: Missing prediction_config_id. Complete Step 2 config first.

step4.panelKeyFacts: Latest key facts from temporal memory
step4.historicalReportNotice: You are viewing a historical report that is no longer the current active project flow. You can review it, but regenerate Step 4 before continuing Q&A.
step4.logStep4Regenerated: Step 4 report regenerated; old Step 5 Q&A is invalidated
step4.drawDetail: Model draw probability. Higher draw probability means guarding against low-score outcomes.
step4.injuryImpact: Missing starters can move the scoreline toward the opponent or draw.
step4.weatherImpact: Bad weather usually lowers tempo and total goals.
step4.cardsImpact: Red cards or penalties can quickly change W/D/L direction.
step4.fitnessImpact: Fitness drop raises late conceded-goal probability.
step4.lineupRecorded: Expected lineups recorded for both teams
step4.lineupImpact: Key-position changes affect shape and matchups.
step4.dataGapImpact: More data gaps mean the highest probability should not be treated as certainty.
step4.sectionHintConclusion: Match judgment, score tendency, and core rationale
step4.sectionHintBasics: Team data, injuries, head-to-head context, and evidence sources
step4.sectionHintTactics: Coach discussion, expected lineups, and attack-defense matchups
step4.sectionHintScore: Score probabilities, xG, and most likely result
step4.sectionHintMatrix: Scenario weights, match picture, and state combinations
step4.sectionHintEvents: Baseline event chain and key nodes
step4.sectionHintCoach: Review opinions, risk, and tactical explanation
step4.sectionHintRisk: VAR, cards, injury exit, and substitution risks
step4.sectionHintBudget: Data sources, call budget, and degradation chain

step5.predictionEvidenceAvailable: Scoreline probabilities, scenario matrix, match event timeline and report sections are connected
step5.predictionQaDesc: Ask follow-up questions about the prediction report, score hypotheses, scenario spaces and high-volatility risks
step5.chatWithAgent: Chat with any individual in the world
step5.reportAgentDesc: Q&A over the match prediction report, prioritizing persisted scoreline probabilities, scenario matrix, match events and analyst notes
step5.toolScorelineDistributionDesc: Read the most likely score, W/D/L probabilities, xG and scenario-level score candidates
step5.toolScenarioMatrixDesc: Inspect the 3x3 home/away state matrix and six aggregated scenario spaces
step5.toolMatchEventTimelineDesc: Replay structured events such as KICKOFF, TACTICAL_PHASE, SHOT, VAR_CHECK, SUBSTITUTION and FINAL_SCORE_HYPOTHESIS
step5.toolInsightForgeDesc: Aligns real-world seed data with simulation state, combining Global/Local Memory for cross-temporal deep attribution analysis
step5.toolPanoramaSearchDesc: Graph-based BFS algorithm that reconstructs event propagation paths, capturing the full topology of information flow
step5.toolQuickSearchDesc: GraphRAG-based instant query interface with optimized indexing for fast extraction of node attributes and discrete facts
step5.toolInterviewSubAgentDesc: Autonomous interviews that conduct parallel multi-round dialogues with simulated individuals, collecting unstructured opinion data and psychological states
step5.chatEmptyReportAgent: Chat with the Prediction Report Assistant to inspect scoreline, scenario and risk evidence
step5.chatEmptyAgent: Chat with simulated individuals to understand their perspectives
step5.surveyInputPlaceholder: Enter the question you want to ask all selected targets...
step5.selectAgentFirst: Please select a simulated individual first
step5.defaultDemoQuestion: If you were the newly appointed Tunisia head coach, what tactical and personnel plans would you make to overcome Japan despite unfavorable expectations?
step5.logStep5Regenerated: Step 5 Q&A regenerated; old active Q&A session is invalidated
step5.qaContextNotInitialized: Prediction Q&A reads persisted football prediction evidence, but the current report conversation could not be initialized.

graph.graphMemoryRealtime: GraphRAG short/long-term memory updating in real-time
graph.pendingContentHint: Some content is still processing. Consider refreshing the graph manually later.
graph.waitingGraphData: Waiting for graph nodes and relationships

history.title: Prediction History
history.replayHint: Completed predictions can replay Step 1-5; interrupted runs can resume from the latest node

api.dbNotExist: Database does not exist. The simulation may not have run yet.
api.missingGraphIdEnsure: Missing graph ID. Please ensure the graph has been built.
api.reportGenerateStarted: Report generation task started. Query progress via /api/report/generate/status.
api.noReportForSim: No report found for this simulation: {id}
api.reportProgressNotAvail: Report not found or progress unavailable: {id}
api.envNotRunning: Simulation environment not running or closed. Ensure simulation is complete and in command-wait mode.
api.envRunning: Environment is running and ready for Interview commands

report.sectionConflict: Section {title} round {iteration}: LLM output both tool call and Final Answer (conflict #{conflictCount})
report.sectionConflictDowngrade: Section {title}: {conflictCount} consecutive conflicts, downgrading to truncate and execute first tool call

workflow.stepImpact1: Regenerating Step 1 will invalidate the current Step 2 config, Step 3 simulation, Step 4 report, and Step 5 Q&A. Historical results are kept, but they will no longer be the current project result. Continue?
workflow.stepImpact2: Regenerating Step 2 will invalidate the current Step 3 simulation, Step 4 report, and Step 5 Q&A. Historical results are kept, but they will no longer be the current project result. Continue?
workflow.stepImpact3: Regenerating Step 3 will invalidate the current Step 4 report and Step 5 Q&A. Historical results are kept, but they will no longer be the current project result. Continue?
workflow.stepImpact4: Regenerating Step 4 will invalidate the current Step 5 Q&A. Historical results are kept, but they will no longer be the current project result. Continue?
workflow.stepImpact5: Regenerating Step 5 will clear the current active Q&A session. Historical Q&A is kept, but it will no longer be the current project result. Continue?
workflow.defaultImpact: Regenerating will invalidate this step and downstream results. Continue?

prediction.dataSourcesTooltip: Shows data snapshots used or attempted by Step 2. Synced data contributes to team-strength estimation; failed or disabled sources fall back to cache, roster, and graph evidence. Expand each row for etag, fetch time, URL, and errors.
prediction.rosterSummaryTooltip: Shows roster coverage, average ability, best goalkeeper rating, unavailable players, and doubtful players for both teams. Average ability feeds team and positional-depth estimates; goalkeeper is shown separately because shot-stopping, distribution, and aerial control enter a distinct goalkeeper dimension.
prediction.budgetProfile_max_detail: For important matches or fuller explanations. Enables all coach roles, two discussion rounds, all-scenario narration, all analyst notes, and post-event review; wait time and model cost are usually highest.
prediction.budgetProfile_custom_detail: Control roles, rounds, narration, notes, MC samples, and hard cap. Defaults to the maximum config and is useful for tuning a deep profile.
prediction.roleEnableTooltip: Choose which coach roles join LLM review. More roles give fuller tactical coverage but increase calls; disabled roles still get agent profiles, they just skip expensive discussion.
prediction.discussionRoundsTooltip: How many coach discussion rounds each enabled role joins. One round is fast; two or three rounds add rebuttal and revision for complex or contentious matches.
prediction.llmDataExtractionTooltip: Let the LLM extract injuries, tactics, weather, competition format, and other unstructured signals from graph text. Turning it off is faster but relies more on existing structured fields and rule parsing.
prediction.narrativePolishTooltip: Choose how many scenarios are rewritten by the LLM as natural match situations. Higher values improve report readability; 0 keeps template summaries only.
prediction.analystNoteSpacesTooltip: Select uncertainty spaces for extra analyst notes. baseline is the base judgment; upside/error/volatility explain upside, error, and high-volatility paths.
prediction.postCoachReviewTooltip: After events are generated, a few coach roles check whether the results align with earlier judgments. This reduces obvious contradictions but adds calls per review role.
prediction.mcSamplesTooltip: Number of match-process simulations. More samples stabilize scoreline distribution and probabilities, but run slower; 500 is for preview, 2000-3000 is usually stable enough.
prediction.statsBombTooltip: Try to use StatsBomb or locally available xG/event data for chance quality. Missing data is skipped automatically and will not block config generation.
prediction.hardCapTooltip: Maximum LLM calls allowed for this config, from 1 to 130. Deep and maximum custom profiles need higher caps; otherwise downstream LLM steps fall back to templates or cache.
prediction.customPreviewNote: More enabled items usually increase latency and model cost
prediction.callDistributionTooltip: fresh is real LLM calls in this run, cached is reused cache hits, and remain is calls left before the hard cap. Planned value comes from the current budget config; actual 0 only means no real calls have happened yet or LLM is disabled.
prediction.lineupHoverTitle: Hover or click a player for details
prediction.lineupHoverText: Position, role, availability, and key attributes appear here without covering the pitch.
prediction.inferredFormationNote: {team} formation is not specified; inferred from position distribution
prediction.datasetIncompatible: Not compatible with this match domain; not recommended
